result.relax_verdict: MIXED — of the 50 killed owned verdicts, ~35 are genuine cross-source adjudications wrongly killed by TEMPLATE rigidity (not faithfulness): they are the ESTABLISHES / DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH / REMAINS_UNRESOLVED shape — operations the module already declares in OPERATIONS (synthesis_contract.py:64-66) and blesses in VERDICT_VOCAB (74-78) but that classify_claim cannot see (no pattern in _CLAIM_PATTERNS 513-537) — plus phrase near-misses ("differ IN unit of analysis" vs the literal "different units of analysis", "cannot resolve/settle" vs "cannot distinguish"). These are exactly the INSIGHT the rubric pays for (two-premise, span-honest, no number/entity/mechanism). The remaining ~15 are topic-sentence framing correctly killed and would still fail the anchoring floor (validate step 10) or the smuggle/mechanism gates on their merits even if classified. So it is worth saving, but only ~70%; the fix must be additive proof rules, not a loosened gate.
sacred_keep: report_ast.py:1295-1301 NUMBER_OR_UNIT_NOT_IN_SPAN / NUMBER_NOT_IN_SPAN — every (num,unit) in an attributed clause must stand alone in the span with the same unit; the literal anti-fabrication requirement
synthesis_contract.py:372-375 synthesis_carries_a_digit / _spelled_quantity — a synthesis/owned verdict may carry NO digit or spelled quantity, ever; this alone rejects self-test #2/#4/#5
report_ast.py:1334-1337 JUDGE_NOT_ENTAILED — whole-clause-vs-span judge; only ENTAILED admits, NOT_ENTAILED/UNCERTAIN/unreachable all fail closed (catches sign-flip, magnitude, scope, modality)
report_ast.py:1768-1772 SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT and 1811-1815 OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE — model prose may name no corpus surname/venue; attribution is rendered from the graph only
report_ast.py:1820-1825 OWNED_ATTRIBUTES_A_FINDING — closes the oblique 'the Cambridge team found' group-word/proper-noun attribution bypass
synthesis_contract.py:426-436 FABRICATED_BINDING — a named author's asserted mechanism must be stated by that author's OWN premise, not another paper in the room
synthesis_contract.py:416-422 causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism — no imported mechanism; rejects self-test #1

synthesis_contract.py:206-230 (invoked 476-478) UNPROVED_RIDER_CLAUSE and report_ast.py:1884-1887 SYNTHESIS_SMUGGLES_A_FINDING — the two independent lines against a smuggled first-order finding; frame judge fails closed on EMPIRICAL/UNCERTAIN/unreachable

synthesis_contract.py:377-407 new_entity / caps_earned (144-161) mirrored at argument_planner.py:1109-1116 OWNED_NEW_ENTITY calling the SAME predicate — no new capitalized entity; rejects self-test #3

synthesis_contract.py:358-361 premise_not_admitted and report_ast.py:1836-1839 PREMISE_UNRESOLVED — premises must be already-admitted cards, re-resolved against the graph at validation, span facets re-derived not trusted

synthesis_contract.py:364-369 AND prove() 566-570 premises_share_a_single_source — SACRED for genuinely relational ops (CONVERGES/CONTRASTS_*/RECONCILES/RANK_EVIDENCE/ESTABLISHES): dropping it lets one paper corroborate itself; keep the enforcement in prove() even if validate's copy is realigned
synthesis_contract.py:439-442 forecast_or_prediction / universal_overclaim — rejects self-test #5 (via digit backstop) and #6

publisher.py:154-193 release boundary — re-runs validate_report on the whole AST, re-resolves every span+attribution against a graph it loads itself, refuses any released sentence absent from the sidecar; SEALED 0o555 write gate (:92-105)
ghost_relax: OWNED_VERDICT_UNCLASSIFIABLE — report_ast.py:1846-1851 (and the ProvenVerdict factory report_ast.py:188-190). Pure fail-closed-on-unrecognized-prose refusal fired when classify_claim (synthesis_contract.py:540-547) returns ('','') against _CLAIM_PATTERNS (513-537). Asserts nothing about spans/numbers/sources/premises — the exact relaxation target. Relax by ADDING the ESTABLISHES/DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH/REMAINS_UNRESOLVED patterns plus their prove() arms, and by loosening phrase-brittle near-misses ('differ in unit of analysis', 'cannot resolve/settle'), so the sentence enters the proof pipeline on its merits.

SYNTHESIS_REFUSED:premises_share_a_single_source for BOUNDARY ONLY — validate() step 3 (synthesis_contract.py:364-369) computes relational = operation not in ('COVERAGE_GAP',), so a legitimate single-source BOUNDARY (pure limitation, relates no two findings) is killed, yet prove() (566-570) already exempts both BOUNDARY and COVERAGE_GAP. Safe to add BOUNDARY to validate's non-relational set to match prove(). CAUTION: this exemption stays SACRED for every genuinely relational operation.
result.bug1_cause: (b) the splitter over-splits at publish time — NOT the node emitting a fragment. The work.venue string carries a trailing PDF-extraction period ("Cureus Journal of Business and Economics.", recorded at outputs/event_ledger.jsonl:1721). render_attribution (report_ast.py:514 in HEAD) splices that raw venue into the LEAD preamble (report_ast.py:589 'Writing in {venue_the} in {year}, {who} show that '), _venue_the (616) fixes only the leading article, so _fmt_sentence emits ONE valid sentence with a period mid-string. The sidecar (report_ast.py:2018 render, sentence_hash 2012) hashes the WHOLE formatted sentence — never split. The publish-check (publisher.py:185-192) re-splits each rendered line with _sentences() (publisher.py:230, regex (?<=[.!?])(\s+)); '. ' after "Economics" is a boundary and "Economics" is in neither _AB nor _IN allow-list (231-234), so it orphans 'Writing in the Cureus Journal of Business and Economics.' whose hash is absent from known -> RefusedToPublish (publisher.py:190). It slipped validate_report because that gate splits the bare finding cl.text, not the "Writing in..." preamble. Both sides share identical \s+/strip normalization, so the mismatch is purely segmentation, not hashing.
result.bug1_fix: Strip periods that end or precede whitespace from the venue at the single point it enters attribution prose, so sidecar segmentation == publish-check segmentation. Exact one-line change at scripts/report_ast.py:658: `venue = re.sub(r'\.(?=\s|$)', '', (work.venue or '').strip()).strip()` replacing HEAD's `who, venue = _who(work), (work.venue or '').strip()` (keep the separate _who(work) assignment). This collapses "Cureus Journal of Business and Economics." and "Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci." to boundary-free labels. It touches ONLY the display label attached after the model writes — span bytes, hashes, attribution resolution, and every guard check are untouched, so it does not weaken the anti-fabrication moat. render_attribution is the only prose path that splices venue into a non-table line (the table path report_ast.py:1997/_table_md emits '| ... |' rows the check skips), so no other emit site needs the guard. IMPORTANT: the fix is present in the working tree but STILL UNCOMMITTED (git status shows `M scripts/report_ast.py`); it must be committed and adversary-verified, not left staged.
result.safety_invariant: Classification may only ROUTE, never ADMIT: a sentence that stops hitting OWNED_VERDICT_UNCLASSIFIABLE must still be forced, unchanged, through the entire existing pipeline. Concretely the fallback must be a TYPED RELATION over already-admitted premises that (1) still passes validate() steps 4-11 (no digit/spelled quantity, no new entity via caps_earned, no imported mechanism, no fabricated binding, no forecast/universal, >=2 anchors in its own premises, no unproved rider clause); (2) maps to a real OPERATIONS entry that has a real prove() branch checking SPAN-BOUND preconditions (shared outcome, span-bound facets, and >=2 premises from >=2 DISTINCT sources — the two-source condition droppable ONLY for BOUNDARY/COVERAGE_GAP); and (3) still passes the owned ProvenVerdict checks (operation-vs-claim non-drift PROVEN_VERDICT_OPERATION_MISMATCH, prove(), and the SYNTHESIS_SMUGGLES_A_FINDING frame judge). Because every one of the 9 invalid self-test cases dies on step 3/4/5/6/7/9/10 — all of which sit BELOW classification and are untouched by widening the recognizer — the invariant guarantees they stay rejected. CRITICAL corollary: adding ESTABLISHES/DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH/REMAINS_UNRESOLVED to classify_claim is necessary BUT NOT sufficient — prove() has no arm for them (falls to `no proof rule for operation`, synthesis_contract.py:731, fail-closed). The matching span-checked prove() rule MUST land in the SAME commit; a catch-all fallback that skips prove() would convert fail-closed into fail-OPEN and is forbidden.
result.go_no_go: GO — for BOTH the venue-strip fix (display-only, guards untouched, fixes a real RefusedToPublish; commit + adversary-verify) and the classifier relaxation, but the relaxation is GO ONLY as an ADDITIVE change: add the three orphaned operation patterns (ESTABLISHES/DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH/REMAINS_UNRESOLVED) and the phrase near-misses to classify_claim, each shipping with a matching span-bound prove() rule (with >=2-distinct-source enforcement, droppable only for BOUNDARY) in the SAME commit, plus the safe BOUNDARY realignment of validate() step 3 to prove()'s non_relational set — and only if the self-test stays 9 invalid / 0 false-admit, 5 valid / 0 false-reject and is adversary re-verified. The one deciding reason: classify_claim is PURE ROUTING that sits upstream of and is independent from every anti-fabrication guard (validate steps 4-11, prove's span-bound preconditions, the entailment judge, the smuggle/rider lines, and the publisher re-validation), so a newly-recognized verdict is still forced through every unchanged proof obligation — widening the recognizer admits real cross-source insight without opening any fabrication path, whereas a prove()-skipping catch-all fallback would be NO-GO because it turns fail-closed into fail-open.
